_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Skill in building and maintaining data service catalogs that document available data products, access methods, service level expectations, ownership, and support channels for data consumers.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI OP domain. Skill OP-S-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-T-021 (AI, cosine: 0.446), OP-K-007 (OP, cosine: 0.429).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.675). Shared vocabulary: [maintaining, skill]. NICE-only terms [directory, services] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, available, building, catalogs, channels, consumers] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.883 (P=0.918, R=0.851). P exceeds R by 0.068 — the NICE element's 5 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 25 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.2%). "Skill in maintaining directory services" and "Skill in building and maintaining data service catalogs that document available" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.563 < STS 0.675 (gap -0.112). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [directory, services] and [access, available, building] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.0%. Shared tokens: [maintaining, skill]. NICE-only: [directory, services]. WIDAI-only: [access, available, building, catalogs, channels, consumers].
